talks.title: Understanding AI Concepts and How to Infuse AI into Your Business Processes
talks.speaker_text: Kait Arnold, Kaitlyn Arnold, Kaitlyn Arnold Kait Arnold
talks.description: **Topic repeated at 2 different times, please join the session that is best suited for your time zone.** In this session, you will learn all about the buzz words surrounding Artificial Intelligence - Generative AI, Large Language Models, Natural Language Processing, and Machine Learning and how these concepts can be weaved into your business processes to transform the future of your organization. Topics Covered: * How Conversational AI is transforming client services augmenting client facing reps via an omni-channel virtual agent * How AI developers can leverage our next generation enterprise studio that empowers users to train, validate, tune, and deploy both traditional and new generative AI capabilities in a low-code/no-code environment * How AI can reduce manual labor of your call center and operations team through call summarization, extraction of key insights, and sentiment analysis **Presenter: Kaitlyn Arnold** Kait Arnold is an AI Solutions Engineer at IBM serving the Financial Services Industry. Prior to IBM, she managed a team of Analytics Consultants working on projects related to business intelligence, data science, and data engineering. She has her Master’s in Computational Mathematics & Statistics in Data Science from the University of Notre Dame and graduated from Fairfield with a dual major in Finance and Information Systems and a minor in Business Analytics. Through her experience, she has cultivated a strong passion for helping others use technology, creativity, and innovation to achieve their goals. Kait enjoys giving back to her community in the form of teaching. In 2022, she taught Python for Data Science for Women in Data, and is currently teaching her first University level Master’s course on Artificial Intelligence at her alma mater, Fairfield University. \*\*\* **Please join us at the session that is best suited to your time zone. Note that this topic is:** **1\. Repeated at two different times to accommodate various time zones\, because it is** **2\. Posted simultaneously in multiple meetup groups world\-wide** \*\*\* It is recommended that you register at this Webex link ahead of time to receive a calendar invite and reminder. **[https://ibm.webex.com/weblink/register/r43f6234331b1d4bcde003494342734d6](https://ibm.webex.com/weblink/register/r43f6234331b1d4bcde003494342734d6)**

talks.event_title: Unique risks of Large Language Models and how to use them more responsibly
talks.title: Unique risks of Large Language Models and how to use them more responsibly
talks.speaker_text: Gautam Siwach @ open source technologies at IBM, Gautam Siwach Gautam Siwach
talks.description: **Topic repeated at 2 different times, please join the session that is best suited for your time zone.** Large Language Models (LLM) have inspired many people because it was able to so succinctly demonstrate to everyone, irrespective of role, the power of the conversational interface. The developers of many of these LLMs have been upfront with the limitations, by including warning labels about bias and unintended uses. The realization of what AI is now doing to transform the world is becoming clearer and clearer for better or for worse. This session will help you understand more on where potential harm could come from as well as ways to mitigate it. **Presenter: Gautam Siwach** Gautam Siwach is a distinguished thought leader and principal executive for open source technologies at IBM. Gautam has published several papers in top-tier conferences and journals and has also served as a reviewer for various IEEE and ACM conferences. As a passionate advocate of Generative Artificial Intelligence, Gautam has delivered talks and presented his research at various international conferences and meetups. He believes that the use of Generative AI techniques can revolutionize the field of robotics and enable safe and reliable interactions between humans and machines. \*\*\* **Please join us at the session that is best suited to your time zone. talks.title: User Group Days - Community Event
talks.description: This event is Eastern Daylight Time. Please adjust to your timezone. To attend the event and view replays, you must register here: https://bit.ly/2yUjVeS This time, users take the stage! Experience a 2-Day Event Featuring 96 Hours of Content from IBM Customers and Experts. User Groups include: AI, Automation, Cloud Integration, Cloud Pak for Data, Cognos Common, DataOps, Data Science, Db2, IBM Security, Informix Maximo, NETCOOL, Netezza, Open Source, Power Systems Public Cloud, Storage, Websphere, Z Systems. We are uniting over a dozen IBM User Groups for a 2-day virtual conference with 6 tracks each day featuring lessons, demos, keynotes, feedback sessions, expert advice, and more. Choose from a comprehensive selection of sessions presented by IBM professionals, partners, customers, and users from around the world who are excited to share their knowledge and expertise with you. Learn more about the technologies you use every day, start a conversation, ask questions, or find new User Groups to connect with. Hashtag:#IBMUserGroupDay Register for this event here: https://bit.ly/2yUjVeS We look forward to hearing from you.

talks.event_title: Using ML to optimize business outcomes
talks.title: Using ML to optimize business outcomes
talks.description: **Please join the session that is best suited to your time zone...this topic is repeated at 2 different times.** This session will provide an introduction to applications of machine learning to optimization. Optimization (often called prescriptive analytics) is a branch of data science that recommends the best actions for maximizing a desirable outcome (or minimizing an undesirable outcome). Modern applications often involve a combination of machine learning and mathematical programming. Attendees will get an introduction to modern applications of prescriptive analytics, illustrated through a variety of real world use cases. These use cases include optimizing treatments to maximize health outcomes, optimizing pricing to maximize profits, and optimizing maintenance operations to minimize cost. A review of these real world applications will enable attendees to explore how prescriptive analytics might contribute value to their own organizations. **Presenter:** **Mark Freeman** Mr. Freeman is an executive data scientist with PhD-level education and over 25 years experience in advanced analytics and machine learning. As a Chief Data Scientist at IBM Consulting, he leads data science teams delivering production grade machine learning solutions to clients across multiple industries. He is a published author of advanced analytics research and principal patent author for optimal automated forecasting. \*\*\* **Please join us at the session that is best suited to your time zone. Note that this topic is:** **1\. Repeated at two different times to accommodate various time zones\, because it is** **2\. Posted simultaneously in multiple meetup groups world\-wide** \*\*\* It is recommended that you register at this Webex link ahead of time to receive a calendar invite and reminder. **[https://ibm.webex.com/weblink/register/r35a4b43d09dd200b049cf51dd4fce7c5](https://ibm.webex.com/weblink/register/r35a4b43d09dd200b049cf51dd4fce7c5)**

talks.event_title: Using Open Source AI w/ Drones to identify humans.... Friendly Cylons 1.0...
talks.title: Using Open Source AI w/ Drones to identify humans.... Friendly Cylons 1.0...
talks.description: AI drones have invaded Strata Data Conference. Attend this meetup and learn how to create your own Friendly Cylons. Trevor will show you how he hacked the drones and connected the camera feed to Machine Learning model to identify humans! This project uses all open source components and will be available on GitHub so we can create an army of Cylons. This project uses IBM Data Science Experience to build the Spark model and HDP open projects like Flink, Solr and Kafka to bring Cylons to life! Watch out New York! Presenter: Trevor Grant, IBM https://secure.meetupstatic.com/photos/event/b/4/f/1/600_464326321.jpeg Bio: Trevor Grant is committer on the Apache Mahout project and Open Source Technical Evangelist at IBM. He holds an MS in Applied Math and an MBA from Illinois State University. Trevor is an organizer of the newly formed Chicago Apache Flink Meet Up, and has presented at Flink Forward, ApacheCon, Apache Big Data, and other meetups nationwide. YOU DO NOT HAVE TO BE REGISTERED FOR STRATA DATA CONFERENCE TO ATTEND THIS MEETUP. This meetup is hosted by IBM and Hortonworks. Light refreshments will be served.

talks.event_title: Watsonx Day, an IBM TechXchange Virtual Event
talks.title: Watsonx Day, an IBM TechXchange Virtual Event
talks.description: Watsonx Day, is an IBM TechXchange Virtual Event and a must-attend event for technologists who want to put AI to work. This free, virtual conference is happening on 21 June 2023 (replays available) across three time zones (ET, EMEA, APAC). * Eastern Time (UTC -5): 11 AM–6 PM * Western European Time (UTC +1) : 3 PM–5 PM * APAC Time (UTC +9): 9 AM–11 AM Access over 40 sessions across three tracks: * **watsonx from IBM Experts:** Top IBM experts share insights and expertise to scale and accelerate the impact of AI with trusted data. * **watsonx from Real World Examples**: IBM users who have successfully implemented watsonx will engage in interactive discussions. * **watsonx from the Ecosystem:** Trusted IBM ecosystem partners will leverage and complement the watsonx platform to drive innovation & AI adoption. Partners will implement and extend watsonx for different use cases and industries, showcasing how it can transform businesses and create value. Watsonx Day provides a sampling of technical content to come at the upcoming [IBM TechXchange Conference](https://www.ibm.com/community/ibm-techxchange-conference/?utm_source=drape&utm_medium=web&utm_campaign=techxchange&utm_id=tech005) in Las Vegas this September. Register for watsonx Day via **[https://ibm-techxchange.bemyapp.com/watsonx](https://ibm-techxchange.bemyapp.com/watsonx)**

talks.event_title: Web-scraping and sentiment analysis for enhanced data analytics & ML modelling
talks.title: Web-scraping and sentiment analysis for enhanced data analytics & ML modelling
talks.description: **Note that this event is posted in multiple meetup groups world-wide. If this time is not suitable for your time zone, please join the session offered at the alternate time slot.** When the data you have is not enough to use with your ML models, extracting data from other sources such as the internet become critical to the success of your model's results. Increasingly, sentiment analysis of textual data can provide valuable insights for making effecting decisions and policies. Please join us for this tutorial where you will have the opportunity to learn to scrape data from the web, and how to perform sentiment analysis on the extracted text. To get the most out of this tutorial, you are encouraged to follow along with the instructor, however not a requirement to benefit from the session. The tutorial will be done in a **Watson Studio Jupyter Notebook**, but please feel free to use the **python compiler** of your choice. In preparation, please have libraries **beautifulsoup4** and **nltk** installed beforehand. **Presenter: Jonathan Messiha** Jonathan is a Data Scientist on IBM's Data Science and AI Elite team. Jonathan has earned a Bachelor of IT with a major in Networking and IT Security from Ontech University, and has completed his Master of Management in Artificial Intelligence from Smith School of Business at Queen's University. Jonathan is an enthusiast in AI and process automation with experience writing automated, end-to-end data and AI pipelines. \*\*\* It is recommended that you register at this Webex link ahead of time to receive a calendar invite and reminder. [https://ibm.webex.com/ibm/j.php?RGID=r98beff91221fa6051117e58e31e3ce78](https://ibm.webex.com/ibm/j.php?RGID=r98beff91221fa6051117e58e31e3ce78)

talks.event_title: WebSphere User Group Roadshow: Liberty and Eclipse OpenJ9 JVM performance
talks.title: WebSphere User Group Roadshow: Liberty and Eclipse OpenJ9 JVM performance
talks.description: Get a sneak peek into the world of Liberty and Eclipse OpenJ9 JVM performance! In this 30-minute session, we will introduce the different facets of Java performance and why they matter. We will also provide a sneak peek into the performance of the combination representing IBM’s Java stack (namely, Liberty application server and the OpenJ9 JVM) in comparison with other Java EE stacks. Finally, we will look ahead to the upcoming [WebSphere User Group (WUG) Roadshow](https://community.ibm.com/community/user/wasdevops/blogs/divya-koppolu1/2022/02/15/virtual-wugs-march-2022) sessions planned for the week of March 21 that will get into greater depth on these topics. **Panelists** **Vijay Sundaresan** is Performance Architect at the IBM Toronto Lab responsible for WAS/Java runtime performance. Vijay’s technical background and expertise is in the areas of performance analysis, compilation and virtual machine technology, Java SE and Java EE specifications, as well as hardware optimizations over the past two decades. Vijay was one of the original architects on both the Eclipse OpenJ9 JVM as well as on the Eclipse OMR open source projects. As a graduate student at McGill University Vijay also made contributions to the Soot bytecode analysis framework. Over his 20+ year career at IBM, **Jared Anderson** has been involved in WebSphere foundation products including WebSphere Application Server (both traditional and Liberty) as well as WebSphere eXtreme Scale. Presently he is the WebSphere Performance team lead as well as the Java / Jakarta EE development lead. As the WebSphere Performance team lead, he works with the team to measure, analyze and improve performance of the WebSphere Application Server products. In his Java / Jakarta EE development lead role, he works with the Liberty development team to implement the current and future specification versions. **Kevin Grigorenko** is a senior Java software engineer on the IBM App Platform SWAT team, which provides worldwide, on­site, and remote supplemental product defect support, particularly in critical production situations. He focuses on problem determination and performance tuning for WebSphere Application Server and related stack products and operating systems, including the IBM and HotSpot JVMs, Linux, AIX, Solaris, Windows, z/OS, HP/UX, and IBMi. He is an open source contributor on the Eclipse Memory Analyzer Tool, Wireshark, Eclipse MicroProfile, and OpenLiberty.

talks.event_title: What does ChatGPT mean for business? - Drive disruptive value with Generative AI
talks.title: What does ChatGPT mean for business? - Drive disruptive value with Generative AI
talks.description: **Summary** Discover how, why, and if your business should be leveraging generative AI like ChatGPT, looking at both the promise and perils associated with monetizing this emerging technology. **Register for this Webcast** at: **[https://ibm.webcasts.com/starthere.jsp?ei=1602956&tp_key=ea866bfe71](https://ibm.webcasts.com/starthere.jsp?ei=1602956&tp_key=ea866bfe71)** **During the session, our experts will discuss:** * Innovations like Foundation Models, and Generative AI that drove the phenomenal success of services such as OpenAI’s ChatGPT, and DALL-E. Learn how they differ from existing AI offerings. * Real-world use cases of how enterprises are already deriving business value from Generative AI. * Understand the potential challenges such as bias, hallucination, security, IP, cost, and more. * How enterprises can responsibly and securely deploy these models at scale. Join IBM Consulting as we share our point-of-view around how enterprises can best drive value from Generative AI & Foundation Models in a responsible way and scale it across your business. You will gain an understanding of real-world client examples, business potential, and how to mitigate the pitfalls of this new AI technology. **Key Speakers:** * **Manish Goyal**, VP & Sr. Partner, Global AI & Analytics Leader, IBM Consulting * **Shobhit Varshney**, VP & Sr. Partner, Americas AI Analytics Leader Consulting

talks.event_title: What's the big deal with Data Fabric vs Data Mesh vs Data Lakes?
talks.title: What's the big deal with Data Fabric vs Data Mesh vs Data Lakes?
talks.description: **Please join us at the session that is best suited to your time zone. Note that this topic is:** **1. Repeated at two different times to accommodate various time zones, because it is** **2. Posted simultaneously in multiple meetup groups world-wide** Recently Data Fabric became a new buzz word, offering a better alternative to fighting data silos. But what about Data Lakes? Just 10 years ago, they also promised to bring an end to data silos... You probably also heard about Data Mesh architecture and might be wondering how it compares to Data Fabric. Join this session to learn how Data Fabric can simplify access to data in today’s siloed data landscapes and how Data Mesh architectures help break down Data Lake monoliths and empower domain teams towards owning and serving data. **Presenter: Lena Woolf** Lena is an experienced Senior Technical Staff Member of IBM Watson Knowledge Catalog, Data and AI. Over the course of her IBM career, Lena has made sustained technical contributions to IBM software products and has been recognized as a deep technical expert and a recognized thought leader in the fields of Information Management and Governance. Lena has been at the forefront of many key transformational initiatives, including Cloud Native Transformation strategy and Platform Based Software strategy. Lena regularly speaks at professional conferences. As an inventor, she has contributed to many patents and constantly pushes the boundaries of what’s possible with IBM technology. She has a demonstrated history of effectively transforming customer challenges and business requirements into enterprise grade software. Lena is passionate about providing a gender inclusive workspace where everyone can collaborate to drive innovation and business growth. \*\*\* It is recommended that you register at this Webex link ahead of time to receive a calendar invite and reminder. [https://ibm.webex.com/ibm/j.php?RGID=rb485fa3103ce74a644b2a5a8949e547a](https://ibm.webex.com/ibm/j.php?RGID=rb485fa3103ce74a644b2a5a8949e547a)

talks.event_title: What trust really means and how we can infuse it in our AI systems
talks.title: What trust really means and how we can infuse it in our AI systems
talks.speaker_text: Saishruthi Swaminathan, Saishruthi Swaminathan Saishruthi Swaminathan
talks.description: **Note that this event is posted in multiple meetup groups world-wide. If this time is not suitable for your time zone, please join the session offered at the alternate time slot.** Machine learning models are increasingly being used to inform high-stakes decisions. In this session, we will explore what it means to build a trustworthy AI system. Focus Areas: * What trust means in the AI space and why it is important * Trustworthy AI, Responsible AI, Sustainable AI - what do they all mean? * Basis of building trustworthy AI systems * Understanding the pillars of trustworthy AI and how they add value * Socio-technological challenges * Tools in the space of trustworthy AI * Overview of different regulations, what is out there, why trust is important to comply with regulations **Presenter: Saishruthi Swaminathan** Saishruthi Swaminathan is an advocate for Trustworthy AI and a Data Scientist. She has a Master's in Electrical Engineering specializing in Data Science and a Bachelor's degree in Electronics and Instrumentation Engineering. Her passion is to dive deep into the ocean of data, extract insights, and use AI for social good. She has spoken at over 70 events worldwide over the past three years. In 2021, the Women in AI (WAIE) team recognized her as one of the 100 brilliant women in AI Ethics 2022. She is on a mission to spread the knowledge and experience she acquired in her learning process. \*\*\* It is recommended that you register at this Webex link ahead of time to receive a calendar invite and reminder: [https://ibm.webex.com/ibm/j.php?RGID=r5530d70abbd99cb2465f2eb30525d3f2](https://ibm.webex.com/ibm/j.php?RGID=r5530d70abbd99cb2465f2eb30525d3f2).

talks.event_title: What you need to know about the EU AI Act
talks.title: What you need to know about the EU AI Act
talks.speaker_text: John Bowman, John Bowman John Bowman
talks.description: **Please join the session that is best suited to your time zone...this topic is repeated at 2 different times.** The EU AI Act is a major new piece of legislation that will regulate critical AI systems. It will apply to providers of AI systems placing them in the European Union market and users of AI in the EU. As the text of the Act is being finalised, this session will provide an update on key provisions including how to deploy a risk-based approach and how to design a governance structure that provides appropriate accountability. **Presenters:** **John Bowman** **John Bowman** joined IBM’s Chief Privacy Office in October 2022 as AI Ethics Market Strategy Lead. His role includes advising on enhancements to support regulatory compliance, helping to create a deployment framework, and commercialisation of select IBM CPO assets. Previously, John was a Senior Principal in Promontory, IBM Consulting; and before that John worked at the UK Ministry of Justice where he was Head of EU and International Data Protection Policy. \*\*\* **Please join us at the session that is best suited to your time zone. Note that this topic is:** **1\. Repeated at two different times to accommodate various time zones\, because it is** **2\. talks.event_title: What you need to know about the EU AI Act
talks.title: What you need to know about the EU AI Act
talks.speaker_text: John Bowman, John Bowman John Bowman
talks.description: **Please join the session that is best suited to your time zone...this topic is repeated at 2 different times.** The EU AI Act is a major new piece of legislation that will regulate critical AI systems. It will apply to providers of AI systems placing them in the European Union market and users of AI in the EU. As the text of the Act is being finalised, this session will provide an update on key provisions including how to deploy a risk-based approach and how to design a governance structure that provides appropriate accountability. **Presenters:** **John Bowman** **John Bowman** joined IBM’s Chief Privacy Office in October 2022 as AI Ethics Market Strategy Lead. His role includes advising on enhancements to support regulatory compliance, helping to create a deployment framework, and commercialisation of select IBM CPO assets. Previously, John was a Senior Principal in Promontory, IBM Consulting; and before that John worked at the UK Ministry of Justice where he was Head of EU and International Data Protection Policy. \*\*\* **Please join us at the session that is best suited to your time zone. Note that this topic is:** **1\. Repeated at two different times to accommodate various time zones\, because it is** **2\. Posted simultaneously in multiple meetup groups world\-wide** \*\*\* It is recommended that you register at this Webex link ahead of time to receive a calendar invite and reminder. **[https://ibm.webex.com/weblink/register/rbfc973411368d0952bf9335a8a4393c1](https://ibm.webex.com/weblink/register/rbfc973411368d0952bf9335a8a4393c1)**

talks.event_title: WiDS New York City Regional Conference
talks.title: WiDS New York City Regional Conference
talks.description: Join us at **CUNY Graduate Center** for a day filled with inspiring talks, workshops, and networking opportunities. Whether you're a seasoned data scientist or just starting out, this event is perfect for anyone interested in the world of data science. Learn from industry experts, connect with like-minded individuals, and gain valuable insights into the latest trends in data science. Don't miss this chance to be part of a supportive community of women in the data science field. Mark your calendars and get ready for an unforgettable experience at Women in Data Science - New York City May 2025! WiDS New York City is independently organized by Vanishri Murali, Vasanthi Gopal, Maria Alejandra Sanchez, Paulina Toro Isaza, Calphurnia John, Sneha Varghese, Chuxin Liu, Niki Karanikola, Sheetal Borar, Astha Puri, Angela & Niharika to be part of the mission to increase participation of women in data science and to feature outstanding women doing outstanding work Please register for the event on WiDS so that it is easier to track overall attendance: [Click here to register](https://community.widsworldwide.org/posts/79058858?utm_source=manual) Detailed agenda will be updated soon.

talks.event_title: Winning with Machine Learning
talks.title: Winning with Machine Learning
talks.description: TO CONFIRM YOUR SEAT, YOU MUST REGISTER HERE: New York - April 18th (https://www-01.ibm.com/events/wwe/grp/grp304.nsf/Registration.xsp?openform&seminar=62ARBWES&locale=en_US) Monetizing the data behind your firewall. With the exponential growth in data, 80% of which is unstructured, the business benefit of using machine learning to seek out opportunities is massive. Amongst a survey of large companies, 76% indicated they are targeting higher sales growth with machine learning. Join us to learn how organizations can take machine learning out of the laboratory and into wide-scale, commercial use so that continuous intelligence can be applied throughout the enterprise. We will share some practical tips on how to get started with machine learning as well as IBM’s plans to use machine learning to infuse cognitive capabilities across our private cloud data and analytics portfolio to unlock the value of the data behind your firewall. There will be two specific tracks covered on the day, one will focus on the area of Data Management and the second will focus on Data Analytics. For a more detailed description on each of these tracks please click here for Session Details (https://www-01.ibm.com/events/wwe/grp/grp304.nsf/Additional.xsp?openform&seminar=DA7JP5ES&locale=en_US) Agenda - 10.00am - Welcome and Introductions - 10.15am - Keynote Address Winning with Machine Learning - Daniel Hernandez, VP of Analytics - 10.45am - Session One: Data Management: Utilizing Spark to Hadoop to Fire up Innovation or Data Analytics: Introduction to Machine Learning for IT Executives - 11.30am - Session Two: Data Management: Diversify Your Applications, Not Your Databases or Data Analytics: Data Science Convergence - 12.15pm - Networking Lunch - 13.30pm - Session Three: Data Management: Tackling Data Compliance & Better Insights with a Unified Governance Approach or Data Analytics: Unleashing Apache Spark and Machine Learning on the Data Warehouse - 14.15pm - Summary & Close

talks.event_title: Women in Data Science and Quantum Computing - March 25
talks.title: Women in Data Science and Quantum Computing - March 25
talks.description: Inspired by Women’s History Month and International Women’s Day and Women in Data Science, IBM and its partners are amplifying the work of incredible women trailblazers and contributors to data science and quantum computing. Join us on March 25 in NYC for a day of talks, hands-on workshops, and networking to learn more about IBM’s revolutionary launch into Quantum Computing, the current state of AI and Data Science and meet the women leading the charge. You can join us for the whole day – or for parts of the day. Please register before March 22: https://ibmwomenindatasciencequantum.splashthat.com More info https://developer.ibm.com/opentech/2019/03/17/women-in-data-science-and-quantum-computing-new-york-2019-03-25/

talks.event_title: Women in Data Science - New York City
talks.title: Women in Data Science - New York City
talks.speaker_text: Ana Osorio-Alvarado, Kelsey McDonald, Maria Alejandra Sanchez, Milbert Kiggundu-Bentham, Niki Karanikola, Panel Moderator, Panel Moderator: Vanishri Murali, Shirley Han, Srideepika Jayaraman
talks.description: To attend - you must register here [https://community.widsworldwide.org/posts/wids-new-york-city-regional-conference-2026](https://community.widsworldwide.org/posts/wids-new-york-city-regional-conference-2026) **What to Expect** Land your dream Data Science Job: focused on real‑world career growth in data science Hands‑on technical workshops on Docling and Granite Agents Inspiring technical talks spanning AI, GenAI, healthcare, manufacturing, cybersecurity, sports analytics, and the future of work A career panel featuring professionals from industry and research sharing honest insights, lessons learned, and actionable advice Plenty of opportunities for networking and community building **More Details** Land your dream Data Science Job - Career Workshop by Ana Osorio-Alvarado, Niki Karanikola Learn about: Resume building & interview preparation Navigating early and mid‑career transitions **Hands‑On Technical Workshops:** * Docling Workshop – practical document understanding and AI workflows with Sophia I, and Lillie Ng * Granite Agent Workshop – building agentic AI systems with real use cases with Sanda Htyte and Cara Mulrooney **Featured Talks & Speakers Include** * Data Science in Sports - by Kelsey McDonald * Lessons from Evaluating GenAI Projects - Samar Deen, PhD * Chatbot Personalization in the Wild - Angelina Wang * From Warehouses to Wellness: AI applications across Manufacturing & Healthcare - Parisa Babaali * AI and the Future of Work - Zanele Munyikwa * Strengthening Cyber Defense with AI - Sophia I. **Career Panel** Hear directly from professionals across industry and research, including AI & data science leaders from IBM, Meta and others —sharing insights on skills, career paths, and what it really takes to succeed in the field. Here are our panelists: Maria Alejandra Sanchez, Shirley Han, Srideepika Jayaraman, Milbert Kiggundu-Bentham, Panel Moderator: Vanishri Murali Who Should Attend * Students and early‑career professionals * Experienced data scientists and ML engineers * Researchers, analysts, and AI practitioners * Anyone curious about applying data and AI to real‑world, high‑impact problems

talks.event_title: Women in Data Science - New York City May 2024
talks.title: Women in Data Science - New York City May 2024
talks.speaker_text: Aakanksha Joshi, Amaris Efthimiou, Clinical Decision Support, Digital Labor @ IBM, Eda Kavlakoglu, Graduate Faculty, Informatics Director, Madhavi Chakrabarty, Rutgers Business School, Senior Research Scientist, Vanishri Murali, WiDS Ambassadors, WiDS Ambassadors Srideepika Jayaraman
talks.description: **Welcome to Women in Data Science - New York City May 2024!** **WiDS New York City is independently organized by *Chuxin Liu, Kannu Priya, Maria Sanchez, Niki Karanikola, Paulina Toro Isaza, Ruchi Mahindru, Sandhya Nayak, Sneha Varghese, Srideepika Jayaraman, Thara Palanivel, Vanishri Murali, Vasanthi Gopal* to be part of the mission to increase participation of *women in data science* and to feature outstanding women doing outstanding work.** **Our aim is to inspire, educate and support Women in the field of Data Science, by uplifting, energizing and building long term relationships across companies, partners, communities and universities in the New York and Tri-State region.** Join us at **CUNY Graduate Center** for a day filled with inspiring talks, workshops, and networking opportunities. Whether you're a seasoned data scientist or just starting out, this event is perfect for anyone interested in the world of data science. Learn from industry experts, connect with like-minded individuals, and gain valuable insights into the latest trends in data science. Don't miss this chance to be part of a supportive community of women in the data science field. Mark your calendars and get ready for an unforgettable experience at Women in Data Science - New York City! **All genders are invited to attend all WiDS Worldwide & Regional conference events.**

talks.event_title: Zowe: Modern Z Systems Programming
talks.title: Zowe: Modern Z Systems Programming
talks.description: 5:00-5:30pm Networking 5:30-6:00 Dave Reich | The Observationist 6:00-7:00pm Meet Zowe! - Bring your laptop if you want to follow along. See the first joint effort Open Mainframe platform in action. Experience the tooling that provides leading edge development capabilities for the Z Systems open source community. Learn how all mainframers (millennial and experienced) can exploit this technology to easily interact with the mainframe. This new framework will enable an ecosystem of software solutions intended to provide a simple, intuitive environment for a variety of IT professionals performing administrative, development, test and operation tasks on z/OS. Your Zowe instructor will be Dan Jast (zSystems Technical Specialist & author of the Zowe Lab) along with Ronnie Geraghty (zSystems Dev Ops Technical Specialist) Zowe was recently named as a one of the finalists for the “Best DevOps Open Source Project” category by Devops.com. “The Devops Dozen” is an annual award dedicated to honoring the best devops projects and programmers. Learning Objectives: This lab allows users to get "hands on" experience with the Zowe UI and CLI. Users will go through a set of tasks, exploiting the abilities Zowe has to offer. For more about Zowe: https://zowe.org/home/
